Details: One bench-scale study investigated removal efficiency of coagulation process for chlortetracycline [1526]. Two waters were tested, i.e. distilled water added with kaolin to achieve a turbidity level of 10 NTU and raw river water. Poly-aluminum chloride (PACl) was added as coagulant at dosages of 5 to 60 mg/L. The removal efficiency ranged from 21% to 43% for the distilled water and 38% to 55% for the raw river water.
